diffCoverage.notes: Checked that restore decrypts credentials in-core, returns only non-secret profiles, and that the captured relaunch log carries no token values.

resolvedFindings.resolution: Live relaunch verified by the project owner: the saved account auto-reconnected with no QR; the captured non-secret log shows restored=1 and load_history hydration, with start_qr_login NOT called during restore.

findings.evidence: restore_sessions decrypts each credential inside the core (store layer, keychain master key) and passes it straight into login; only non-secret AccountProfile DTOs are returned to the UI. A per-account login failure is logged with the account id only and the account is marked reauth-needed (no token value logged). The captured relaunch log artifact contains account ids only — no imei/cookie/userAgent values.

summary: Security review (reconciliation): startup restore keeps credentials in-core (decrypt -> login), returns only non-secret profiles, and degrades safely on failure (mark reauth-needed, skip). The captured live relaunch log is free of token values. The earlier needs-human blocker (unrun relaunch) is resolved by the project owner's device run. No blocking findings; pass.
